---
title: "Health and Economic Returns to Dietary Sodium Reduction"
subtitle: "A national-scale cardiovascular disease modeling and benefit--cost analysis"
author: "Resolve to Save Lives (RTSL)"
date: ""
output:
  beamer_presentation:
    latex_engine: xelatex
    keep_tex: false
    slide_level: 2
    toc: false
classoption: "aspectratio=169"
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{booktabs}
  - \usepackage{array}
  - \usepackage{longtable}
  - \usepackage{graphicx}
  - \usepackage{tikz}
  - \usepackage{ragged2e}
  - \usetheme{default}
  - \usecolortheme{default}
  - \setlength{\emergencystretch}{2em}
  - \AtBeginSection[]{}
  # --- Add slide number / total slides (no external package needed) ---
  - \setbeamertemplate{footline}[frame number]
  - \setbeamertemplate{navigation symbols}{}
fontsize: 11pt
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# Overview

## Project Objective and Policy Impact

\justifying

- Estimate national-scale health impacts of dietary sodium reduction policies using a population CVD model.
- Quantify deaths and cases of ischemic heart disease (IHD), stroke, and hypertensive heart disease, as well as DALYs averted over 2026--2050.
- Conduct a full economic benefit--cost analysis to support government decision-making.
- Focus on RTSL partner countries and on policies that governments can realistically implement.

## WHO SHAKE Strategy: Policy Framework

\justifying

- WHO SHAKE provides a comprehensive framework for population-wide sodium reduction.
- Components include:
  - **S**urveillance
  - **H**arness Industry (food reformulation)
  - **A**dopt Standards for labeling and marketing
  - **K**nowledge (consumer information)
  - **E**nvironments (healthier food settings)
- The largest and most sustained sodium reductions occur when SHAKE components are implemented as a package.
- This project explicitly models and costs realistic SHAKE policy bundles.

## RTSL Sodium Reduction Policy Support

\justifying

- Technical assistance to governments to design and implement mandatory sodium targets for packaged foods.
- Support for front-of-pack sodium warning labels to inform consumers and incentivize reformulation.
- Development of nutrition standards for public food procurement in schools, hospitals, and government settings.
- Assistance with policy drafting, legal design, monitoring systems, and enforcement capacity.

## RTSL Sodium Reduction Policy Support: Reference

\centering

\Large \textbf{Background reference}

\vspace{0.6cm}

\normalsize
\url{https://www.nature.com/articles/s41371-021-00612-6}


## Sodium and Potassium: Complementary Strategies for BP Control

- Sodium reduction policies are intrinsically linked to increasing potassium intake, as both nutrients play complementary roles in lowering blood pressure and reducing CVD risk.

- While excess sodium raises BP, potassium promotes vasodilation and enhances urinary sodium excretion.

### Key Aspects of the Relationship
- **Synergistic impact** — Lower sodium combined with higher potassium intake produces larger reductions in blood pressure and stroke risk than sodium reduction alone.
- **Public health strategy** — Many national policies incorporate potassium-enriched salt substitutes (e.g., 75% NaCl / 25% KCl) to simultaneously reduce sodium and address widespread potassium deficiencies.
- **Physiological balancing** — Potassium counteracts sodium’s adverse effects, lowering hypertension risk.
- **Targeted goals** — guidelines emphasize a dual approach: reducing sodium intake and increasing daily potassium consumption.

### Common Policy Approaches
- Promotion of whole, minimally processed foods (fruits, vegetables, legumes) that are naturally high in potassium and low in sodium.


## CVD Modeling Engine

\justifying

- Population cohort model linking sodium intake to blood pressure and cardiovascular outcomes.
- Uses country-specific baseline CVD incidence, mortality, and population age structure.
- Policy scenarios translate sodium reductions into blood pressure changes using established dose--response relationships.
- Resulting changes in heart attacks, strokes, and CVD deaths are projected over time.

## CVD Modeling Engine

\centering

\includegraphics[width=0.88\linewidth]{slide7-img.png}

## Health and Economic Benefits Estimation

\justifying

- Reduced sodium intake leads to fewer cardiovascular events and premature deaths.
- Health outcomes are summarized as deaths and DALYs averted.
- Health gains are monetized using the standard economic valuation of mortality risk reduction.
- Averted health-care expenditures are estimated and included as additional economic benefits.

## Costing the SHAKE Policy Package

\justifying

- Costs are estimated using an ingredients-based regulatory costing approach.
- Government costs include policy design, administration, monitoring, and enforcement.
- Industry costs include reformulation, testing, and labeling compliance, counted as real resource costs.
- Both start-up and ongoing recurrent costs are explicitly modeled over time.

## Costing the SHAKE Policy Package (Example)

\scriptsize

\begin{tabular}{p{2.2cm} p{3.1cm} p{1.7cm} p{1.6cm} p{1.8cm} p{2.3cm}}
\toprule
\textbf{SHAKE Component} & \textbf{Activity} & \textbf{Cost Category} & \textbf{Frequency} & \textbf{Coverage} & \textbf{Cost Bearer} \\
\midrule
S -- Surveillance & Population sodium survey & Program & Every 3--5 yrs & National & Government \\
S -- Surveillance & Food sodium monitoring & Program & Annual & Processed foods & Government \\
H -- Harness Industry & Set reformulation targets & Program & One-off & Key food categories & Government \\
H -- Harness Industry & Product reformulation & Compliance & One-off + transition & Affected SKUs & Industry \\
A -- Labeling & Front-of-pack labeling policy & Program & One-off & National & Government \\
A -- Labeling & Label changes & Compliance & One-off & Affected SKUs & Industry \\
K -- Knowledge & Mass media campaign & Program & Annual & Population reached & Government \\
E -- Environment & School meal standards & Program & Annual & Schools & Government \\
E -- Environment & Institutional food reformulation & Compliance & One-off + annual & Institutions & Vendors \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}

## Integrated Web-Based Decision Tool

\justifying

- All modeling and economic components are integrated into an interactive \texttt{R Shiny} web application.
- Users can customize country-specific inputs, policy coverage, and implementation timelines.
- The tool enables real-time comparison of alternative policy scenarios and assumptions.
- Designed to support evidence-based policymaking and stakeholder engagement.

## Full Benefit--Cost Analysis Outputs

\justifying

- Health benefits and implementation costs are combined to estimate net present value.
- Benefit--cost ratios and return on investment are calculated for each policy scenario.
- Results allow comparison across individual SHAKE components and bundled strategies.
- Sensitivity analyses assess robustness to key assumptions and uncertainties.

## Why This Analysis Matters for RTSL

\justifying

- Provides finance-ministry--ready evidence on the economic value of sodium reduction policies.
- Strengthens regulatory impact assessments and national investment cases.
- Supports prioritization of high-return noncommunicable disease prevention strategies.
- Scalable framework applicable across multiple countries and policy contexts.

## Next Steps

\justifying

- Finalize country-specific data inputs and validate assumptions with national stakeholders.
- Complete deployment and testing of the interactive web tool.
- Use results to support policy adoption, financing, and sustained implementation.
- Position sodium reduction as a high-return national investment in partner countries.
